TextENGNew: I can't die in a place like this!!
I will survive—I have to get back to Yukie!!
For just an instant, I catch a flicker of hesitation in the eyes of the man leveling the gun at me.
changeReason: "holding the gun" (simply possessing) vs "leveling the gun at me" (aiming); the Japanese 構える means to assume a stance/aim, so the new version is more accurate.

TextENGNew: This man is still wavering—he can't bring himself to shoot...?!
Then if I rush him now, ...maybe—!!

changeReason: "bastard" and "fool" are different insults; the Japanese ダラズ is Niigata dialect for fool/idiot, making "fool" more accurate.
"In a flash" emphasizes speed while "Suddenly" (matching 突然) emphasizes unexpectedness; different temporal characterization.

TextENGNew: Everything before my eyes goes dark, ...and my consciousness begins to slip away.
I only understood that I had been shot when my body temperature plummeted and the searing heat I felt in my chest turned numb in an instant...
